# Copilot instructions for luxinchengyan/kids-game

Summary
- Repository currently contains only README.md and .cursorrules. No application source (src/, package.json, etc.) was detected.
- .cursorrules contains important assistant-level guidance (React + Framer Motion + Canvas, Zustand, UI rules) — incorporated below.

Build / test / lint
- No build/test/lint scripts detected in repo. If this becomes a standard React/Node project, typical commands to expect:
  - Install: `npm install` or `pnpm install`
  - Dev server: `npm run dev` or `npm start`
  - Build: `npm run build`
  - Test (full): `npm test`
  - Lint: `npm run lint`
- Running a single test (Jest example): `npm test -- -t "<test name or regex>"` or `npx jest <path/to/test> -t "name"`.

High-level architecture (what Copilot should expect)
- Intended stack (from .cursorrules): React UI, Framer Motion for transitions, HTML Canvas for core game rendering, Zustand for global state.
- Typical layout to look for or suggest to contributors:
  - `src/` — application source
    - `src/components/` — UI components (menus, controls, HUD)
    - `src/game/` — canvas rendering, game loop, physics/logic modules
    - `src/store/` — Zustand stores and selectors
    - `src/styles/` or CSS-in-JS — theme and layout
  - `public/` or `assets/` — images, audio, fonts used by the game
  - `index.html` and a single-page React entry (`src/main.tsx` or `src/index.jsx`)
- Runtime expectations: lightweight single-page app, small asset bundle for fast load on low-end devices.

Key conventions and rules (project-specific)
- UI/UX: Bright colors, extremely responsive interactions; any user action must have visible feedback within 200ms.
- Code style: Prefer functional React components, keep state local where possible, use Zustand for shared/global state.
- Interaction rules: Avoid nested or complex menus; prefer direct, obvious controls tailored for young children.
- Animations: Use Framer Motion for UI transitions; use canvas for the main interactive game surface.
- When generating code: First outline the logic and implementation steps, then output the runnable code (this mirrors .cursorrules requirements).

AI / assistant files found and integrated
- .cursorrules — contains explicit assistant instructions (Chinese). Key lines were incorporated into the "Key conventions" and "High-level architecture" sections above.

When editing this file
- If a different stack or scripts are added (package.json, Makefile, etc.), update the Build / test / lint section with exact commands and how to run a single test.
- If new AI assistant guidelines are added (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, AIDER_CONVENTIONS.md, .clinerules, .windsurfrules, etc.), merge their essential rules here.

Notes for Copilot sessions
- Before generating non-trivial code, scan for `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `vite.config.*`, `webpack.config.*`, `src/` and `public/` to detect the actual stack and scripts.
- Respect the 200ms feedback rule and UI simplicity when suggesting UI/UX code.
